Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree model that is underfitting The current model is a sequence of Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition A parallel block containing Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The issue is that the parallel block allows the activities inside to occur in any order which is not realistic For example you cannot pay an invoice before you receive the goods or match the invoice to the PO before receiving the invoice

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The intended order is Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the parallel block should be a strict sequence

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that there might be some concurrency allowed in real life

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The problem states that we want to enforce a sequence that only allows the normal domain appropriate order So we must make the entire process a strict sequence of all the steps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But wait the problem says retain all the same activities but enforce a sequence and structure that only allows the normal domain appropriate order So we should not change the activities only the structure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore we should replace the parallel block with a sequence of the same activities in the correct order

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the problem says introduce choices only where necessary In the standard Procure to Pay process there might be choices for example if a requisition is rejected or if a goods receipt does not match but the problem states we are simplifying and the normal flow is the strict sequence So we do not need choices here

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the corrected model should be a sequence of all the activities in the given order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The current model has a sequence that starts with create req and approve req then the parallel block and then close case We are going to change the parallel block to a sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So the new structure will be root equals SEQUENCE create req approve req create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we could keep the structure of having a nested sequence for the parallel block part but that is the same

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The original model had a nested structure the parallel block was a child of the top sequence We can keep that nesting by making the inner block a sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Therefore I will present the corrected model as sequence block equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice and root equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create req approve req sequence block close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: By replacing the PARALLEL operator with a SEQUENCE operator for the block containing the activities from creating the purchase order to paying the invoice we enforce that these activities must occur in the exact order specified

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The top level SEQUENCE operator enforces that the entire process must start with creating and approving the requisition then the sequence of the inner block and then closing the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This prevents unwanted behavior such as paying the invoice before receiving the goods or matching the invoice before receiving it
